research.status_summary: Connecteam is actively hiring across sales, product, AI engineering, and customer success roles with no signals of layoffs or acquisition as of June 2026.
research.hiring_policy: US-based remote roles are restricted to specific states (TX, NY, SC, NC, CO, FL, UT, GA) with no public mention of visa sponsorship or EOR friendliness; Israel-side roles are hybrid out of Tel Aviv/Rishon LeZion, making direct EOR engagement unlikely without a local entity.
recent_news: March 4, 2026: Connecteam announced a strategic integration with Accu-Time Systems, connecting their workforce platform with Accu-Time's stride80 biometric/physical time clocks for real-time payroll data sync.
March 2026 product release: Launched a built-in applicant tracking/hiring flow, starred messages in comms, mobile scheduling templates, and Account Safe Mode for suspicious activity.
January 2026: New VP of R&D (Eliran Goldsteine) and a Senior Talent Acquisition Partner joined, signaling deliberate senior-level buildout on both product and recruiting sides.
research.growth_signal: Headcount sits at ~500 employees (Tracxn: 504 as of Feb 2026), up from 311 cited in earlier 2026 estimates, with $157–169M total raised (Series C last in March 2022) and 80,000+ business customers; no new funding round announced but partnership and product velocity remain high.
research.fit_for_lior: Weak fit overall — Connecteam is a deskless/frontline workforce SaaS with US-state-restricted remote roles and an Israel hybrid R&D base; no explicit Product Analyst or Creative AI mandate surfaced, though an open AI Developer role (Tel Aviv hybrid) and Product Manager role could loosely align with her vibe-coding and product threads if she targets the Israel-side pipeline.
